metadata.description: Systematic disassembly and analysis of 16 high-priority goform/ handler functions, tracing all websGetVar (fcn.0002bd4c) calls to dangerous sinks (doSystemCmd, system, strcpy, sprintf)

dangerous_sinks.validation: NONE - strcmp(action, 'del') == 0 branch, but usbName is not validated
dangerous_sinks.path: websGetVar('usbName') -> var_20h -> doSystemCmd('cfm post netctrl %d?op=%d,string_info=%s', 51, 3, usbName)
dangerous_sinks.notes: When action=='del', user-supplied usbName is passed directly as string_info to cfm command via doSystemCmd. No sanitization. Attacker can inject shell metacharacters (;, |, $(), `) to execute arbitrary commands as root.

dangerous_sinks.user_input_param: INDIRECT - guestuser saved to config via SetValue, then retrieved via GetValue
dangerous_sinks.validation: NONE on the stored value, but requires two-step exploitation
dangerous_sinks.path: websGetVar('guestuser') -> SetValue('usb.samba.guest.user') -> CommitCfm -> GetValue('usb.samba.guest.user') -> doSystemCmd('busybox deluser %s')
dangerous_sinks.notes: User-supplied guestuser is first saved to NVRAM config, then on the non-del path the stored value is retrieved and passed to 'busybox deluser %s'. Two-step attack: first save malicious username, then trigger the code path.
Hardcoded integer arguments only, no user input

handlers_analyzed.name: formSetSysToolDDNS
handlers_analyzed.notes: All 25 parameters are passed through SetValue() to NVRAM configuration. No doSystemCmd/system/strcpy calls with user input. The only sprintf writes '{"errCode":%d}' response. send_msg_to_netctrl('advance_type=%d', hardcoded) is used for IPC. NOT VULNERABLE to injection or BOF.

handlers_analyzed.name: formSetSpeedWan
handlers_analyzed.verdict: SAFE - all doSystemCmd calls use hardcoded values, user input only goes to SetValue()

handlers_analyzed.name: formSetDeviceName
handlers_analyzed.notes: Parameters passed to internal function at 0xc7b94 for processing. sprintf writes '{"errCode":%d}' response only. CommitCfm called. No doSystemCmd/system/strcpy with user input.

handlers_analyzed.name: formSetCfm
handlers_analyzed.notes: This is a generic configuration setter. It iterates over name0-name20/value0-value20 pairs and calls SetValue(). When msgtype=='PostMsgToNetctrl', it calls PostMsgToNetctrl(). When funcname=='save_list_data', calls fcn.0004f080. When funcname=='changelanip', calls changelanip(). No doSystemCmd/system. Large attack surface but uses safe APIs.
handlers_analyzed.verdict: SAFE - no command injection, though the generic config-setting nature allows broad config manipulation

handlers_analyzed.name: formSetPPTPServer

dangerous_sinks.validation: sscanf('%[^.].%[^.].%[^.].%s') validates IP format to 4 parts, limiting injection
dangerous_sinks.notes: startIp is parsed via sscanf into 4 dot-separated parts, then reassembled via sprintf. The sscanf parse limits each part but does not enforce length. However, the reassembly into a 128-byte buffer from 4 small parts makes overflow unlikely.

handlers_analyzed.notes: Most input goes through SetValue(). IP addresses are parsed via sscanf and validated to have 4 dot-separated parts. send_msg_to_netctrl used with hardcoded format. No doSystemCmd with user input.
handlers_analyzed.verdict: LOW RISK - sscanf provides minimal format validation

handlers_analyzed.name: formSetQosBand
handlers_analyzed.notes: The 'list' parameter is processed through a parsing function (0x7eb18) then passed to set_wl_guest_qos_list(). doSystemCmd uses only hardcoded values. No user input reaches dangerous sinks.

handlers_analyzed.name: fromSetRouteStatic
handlers_analyzed.notes: The 'list' parameter is passed to a parsing function (0x79160) which processes route entries. CommitCfm is called, then send_msg_to_netctrl with hardcoded 'advance_type=%d'. No doSystemCmd/system/strcpy with user input.

handlers_analyzed.name: mDMZSetCfg
handlers_analyzed.notes: dmzEn and dmzIp are validated (inet_addr check for IP), then saved via SetValue('wan1.dmzen'/'wan1.dmzip'). send_msg_to_netctrl with hardcoded format. No doSystemCmd/system/strcpy with user input.

handlers_analyzed.name: formSetSafeWanWebMan
handlers_analyzed.notes: All parameters saved via SetValue(). send_msg_to_netctrl used with hardcoded format. IP validation via helper function (0xb7c44). No doSystemCmd/system/strcpy with user input.

handlers_analyzed.name: formSetVirtualSer
handlers_analyzed.notes: The 'list' parameter is passed to parsing function (0x77488). CommitCfm called, then send_msg_to_netctrl with hardcoded format. No doSystemCmd/system/strcpy with user input.

handlers_analyzed.name: formWanParameterSetting
handlers_analyzed.notes: Module parameter selects wan1/wan2 config path. Calls sub-function (0x70858) for actual configuration. No doSystemCmd/system/strcpy with user input in this handler.

handlers_analyzed.name: formSetClientState

dangerous_sinks.notes: atoi(limitEn), limitSpeed, limitSpeedUp, deviceId assembled via sprintf into buffer. Buffer is large (fp-0x224 ≈ 548 bytes) relative to typical input sizes.

handlers_analyzed.verdict: LOW RISK - doSystemCmd uses hardcoded args, sprintf buffer appears adequately sized

handlers_analyzed.name: formSetSchedLed
handlers_analyzed.notes: All parameters saved via SetValue(). No doSystemCmd/system/strcpy with user input. Uses send_msg_to_time_check for IPC.

handlers_analyzed.name: formSetMacFilterCfg
handlers_analyzed.notes: Parameters passed to parsing functions (0xc1f0c, 0xc2318). snprintf used for 'op=%d,wl_rate=%d' with hardcoded values sent to send_msg_to_netctrl. No doSystemCmd/system/strcpy with user input.

handlers_analyzed.name: fromNatStaticSetting
handlers_analyzed.notes: Parameters used with SetValue() and parsing functions. PostMsgToNetctrl used with internal data. No doSystemCmd/system/strcpy with user input.

handlers_not_found.notes: No formSetAutoPing handler exists in AC15 httpd. Ping functionality uses simple_ping() library call, not system('ping ...'). The firewall ping setting (firewall.pingwan) is set via formSetFirewallCfg which is already documented.
The remote web management handler is named formSetSafeWanWebMan in the binary. Analyzed above.
PPPoE configuration is handled by formWanParameterSetting which dispatches to sub-handlers. Analyzed above.
Static IP/NAT configuration uses fromNatStaticSetting. Analyzed above.
Port forwarding uses formSetVirtualSer. Analyzed above.
DMZ configuration uses mDMZSetCfg. Analyzed above.
No dedicated formSetNetControl handler. Network control is distributed across formSetClientState, formSetQosBand, and formSetMacFilterCfg.
No formSetURLFilter handler found. URL filtering may be handled through aspTendaGetStatus or formSetFirewallCfg.

new_findings.title: Command Injection in formSetSambaConf via usbName Parameter
new_findings.notes: The usbName HTTP parameter is passed directly to doSystemCmd without any sanitization when action=='del'. Shell metacharacters in usbName will be executed as root.

new_findings.title: Stored Command Injection in formSetSambaConf via guestuser Parameter
new_findings.path: Step 1: POST /goform/SetSambaConf action=set&guestuser=PAYLOAD (stored to NVRAM 'usb.samba.guest.user'). Step 2: Next invocation retrieves stored value via GetValue() and passes to doSystemCmd('busybox deluser %s', stored_value)
new_findings.notes: Two-step stored injection. The guestuser value is saved to NVRAM first, then retrieved and used in a busybox deluser command on subsequent calls. No sanitization at either step.

summary.key_observation: Most Tenda AC15 handlers follow a safe pattern: websGetVar -> SetValue -> CommitCfm -> send_msg_to_netctrl(hardcoded). The vulnerability in formSetSambaConf is an exception where user input flows directly to doSystemCmd via the string_info parameter of a cfm command. The formSetAutoPing handler does not exist in this firmware - ping uses simple_ping() library function.

common_sinks.SetValue: NVRAM setter - safe for injection, but allows arbitrary config changes
common_sinks.doSystemCmd: Shell command execution - dangerous when format string has %s from user input
common_sinks.send_msg_to_netctrl: IPC message - generally uses hardcoded formats, safe
common_sinks.PostMsgToNetctrl: IPC message - uses internal data, safe
common_sinks.CommitCfm: Config commit - no user input passed
